meta.title: The Long Investor — Asset Recommendations Dashboard
meta.subtitle: Elliott Wave analysis from TLI emails (last 30 days) · Assets & collections parsed from email subjects ($TICKER - COLLECTION) · Click any ticker for full TLI summary

assets.summary: BABA hit TLI's buy zone below the 200 WMA and TLI executed as planned, treating the pullback as a natural side effect of the company spending aggressively to fund growth. Price has since reclaimed both the 200 WMA and the $109 0.618 Fib level and is looking strong. TLI would look to add again on a retest of the $103–$109 zone, and continues to view BABA as a useful hedge against the US market this year.

ZETA jumped 5% then extended gains after-hours following CEO comments repositioning the company as an AI infrastructure business, alongside a newly announced Palantir partnership. TLI notes 12 of 14 covering analysts rate the stock a buy, and views the pop as a fresh higher high building on support at the 50-week MA. A break above the $26 range line would confirm the impulse move toward the Wave 3 target of $37.

assets.wave: Wave B (complete) → Wave C pullback, testing 0.5 Fib
assets.entry: $28.60 (0.5 Fib, ABC pullback, capitulation-like) / $24.10 (blue support line + 0.618 Fib + 200 DMA, key downside level) — accumulated $30-$35
assets.target: $60 (1 Fib) → $83 (1.618 Fib, Wave 3, revised up from $58)

levels.label: Wave Count
levels.value: Wave B (complete) → Wave C (awaiting support confirmation)

assets.summary: WYFI pulled back sharply from $48 to $29, right into the zone where TLI had accumulated between $30 and $35, with yesterday's session feeling like capitulation as the 0.5 Fib at $28.60 was tested. TLI reads the July bounce as a three-wave Wave B, now unfolding into a Wave C pullback, and is watching the long-standing blue support/resistance line at $24.10 — which lines up with the 0.618 Fib and rising 200-day MA — as the level that should not be lost. TLI is not selling and reiterates the upside targets of $60 (1 Fib) and a revised $83 (1.618 Fib, Wave 3), but wants support confirmation before treating this as a fresh buy signal.

assets.wave: Wave 3 ↑ (ABC pullback to 0.5-0.618 Fib confluence, testing $12.52 weekly support)
assets.entry: $13.65 (0.5 Fib, daily) / $12.52 (50 WMA + wedge topline + 0.618 Fib, weekly confluence)
assets.summary: ADUR flushed down sharply on no news after losing its 50-day MA at $14.95, a technical, stop-loss-driven drop that pulled price to the 0.5 Fib at $13.65 and toward the 200-day MA — a strong confluence zone TLI treats as a standard three-wave ABC pullback within the larger Wave 3 impulse. On the weekly chart, the 50-week MA, wedge topline, and 0.618 Fib all converge at $12.52, which TLI is confident will hold as support before the next leg higher toward the unchanged $27 target. TLI notes price is back near where the CEO was adding in early June and continues to call ADUR one of the easiest long-term holds in the market given its execution, partnerships, and moat.

GRRR has slipped back below its 50-day moving average at $16.59 after repeatedly testing it, shifting the near-term setup back to watch-and-wait rather than active accumulation. TLI wants to see whether buyers step in on the next test of this level; a rejection would put the 200-day MA in play as the next support to watch for a clean buy signal. The longer-term Wave 3 count is unchanged, with the 1.618 Fib target still at $33.

assets.wave: Wave 2 (testing 0.5–0.618 Fib range after $1B offering-driven drop)
assets.entry: $51–$66 (0.5–0.618 Fib, 50/200 WMA confluence) / $61 (blue support line); prior adds at $56, $69

levels.label: Catalyst / Risk
levels.value: New $1B public offering pressured price (-13% AH, -8% overnight)

assets.summary: ASTS priced another $1 billion public offering, part of a broader wave of capital raises TLI flags as a possible market red flag heading into a seasonally weak stretch, and the stock dropped as much as -13% after hours before settling around -8% overnight. TLI now reads this as a Wave 2 pullback testing the 0.5–0.618 Fib range between $51 and $66, with the blue support line at $61 as the key level to hold. The pullback doesn't change the larger impulse count, and TLI is considering adding again while reiterating this remains a long-term, no-sell holding.

assets.wave: Wave 1 bounce, watching for Wave 2 pullback
assets.summary: TLI groups SE with BABA, MELI, and TOST as emerging-market hedges that have all flipped their 200 WMA to support and broken higher, with SE and MELI currently looking the strongest. SE remains TLI's top pick in the group, bolstered by the added Druckenmiller endorsement. The next test is the 50 WMA at $120, then $148 above that, but TLI still views price as being within the Wave 1 bounce, flagging a Wave 2 pullback retest of the 0.618 Fib at $97 as a clear buy.

PFE has now been added to the Watchlist alongside its Safe Haven tag. TLI says Wave 2 has completed a textbook pullback to the 0.618 Fib near $23, after Wave 1 and 2 together took roughly 18 months, putting the stock into an emerging Wave 3 that isn't tracking the broader market. With a ~7% dividend and FCF-backed valuation, TLI frames it as an attractive defensive entry heading into a historically weak seasonal stretch.

Silver is holding support in a bullish descending wedge at the 0.618 Fib, and TLI sees echoes of last year's rally from $35 to $120. With seasonal weakness approaching and the broader market extended in Wave 5, TLI flags silver's safe-haven appeal and calls $53-$66 the optimal accumulation zone. A flip of the 50 WMA to support would confirm a breakout of the wedge above $66, opening the door to a Wave 5 target as high as $160.

GOOG tested and is holding the confluence of the rising wedge's lower boundary and the 2.618 Fib at $351. If that level holds, TLI's next objective is the 3.618 Fib / upper wedge boundary near $453, which remains the Wave 5 target. TLI flags that rising wedges are typically bearish structures, but believes there is still room for Wave 5 to extend, consistent with its broader market outlook for the coming weeks.

AMZN bounced off the 50 WMA near $232 and closed the week at $245, putting the 2 Fib at $250 in focus next. If price can flip $250 to support, TLI considers AMZN to have entered its Bull Case, turning attention to the Wave 5 target of $290 (2.618 Fib). The key risk flagged is a rejection at $250 that also fails to hold the 50 WMA.

AAPL broke above the $310 2.618 Fib level this week, and the key question now is whether that level flips from resistance to support on a retest. If it holds and the broader market keeps rallying into NVDA earnings and the midterm elections, TLI expects AAPL to keep climbing alongside the market, with the 3.618 Fib at $356 marked as the next bullish target.

MSFT tested resistance at its $388 200 WMA last week but failed to close above it — TLI stresses this is just a test, not yet a confirmed rejection, since a lower low would be needed to confirm one. TLI continues to recommend accumulating below the 200 WMA, viewing MSFT as a core long-term holding likely to outperform the market over the next five years. Downside risk remains to the $344 and $294 Fib levels, which TLI would treat as additional buying opportunities.

SHOP has reclaimed the 0.618 Fib at $113 and is pushing toward the 50 WMA near $182-188. TLI's plan requires flipping the 50 WMA to support and breaking above the Wave 3 high / blue resistance line near $176 to confirm Wave 5 is underway. The Wave 5 target is unchanged at the 2.618 Fib of $227.

assets.wave: Wave C pullback, testing 0.5 Fib support
assets.entry: $106 (0.5 Fib, next test) / $83 (0.618 Fib, downside risk)
assets.summary: PLTR broke back below its $125 support line to $123 amid renewed SaaS-sector weakness triggered by a weak IBM earnings report, putting the 0.5 Fib at $106 back in play. Losing that level would open a drop to the standard pullback zone at the 0.618 Fib near $83, where the rising 200 WMA is also converging. No change to the long-term $180 target or the Buy rating.

BIDU briefly dipped into TLI's load-the-boat range before bouncing to reclaim the 200 WMA at $111, and price is now challenging the 50 WMA at $122 from below. A flip of $122 to support would open the door to the First Target at $130 en route to the larger $200 Wave 3 target.

TLI is calling NVO's price action textbook: the bounce off the 0.78 Fib and 200-monthly MA near $35 has carried straight to the 50-week MA, and a break above it opens the door to the 0.618 Fib at $59 and eventually the 200 WMA at $82. TLI compares the setup directly to how $UNH previously reclaimed its 200 WMA. Anyone who missed the earlier $38 add is told the 50 WMA turning support around $50 is the next sensible entry.

CRM is within reach of the 0.887 Fib at $142, where TLI wants Wave C to complete. Downside risk is seen as limited, with price expected to stabilise in the Buy Zone between the 0.78 and 0.887 Fib before turning up.

assets.entry: $75-$77 (0.5 Fib support) — must hold $70.10 support line

levels.label: Key Support
levels.value: $70.10 (must hold for setup to remain valid)

assets.summary: NFLX reports earnings today after the close, a potential catalyst for the stock's tightening wedge pattern — as long as price holds the $70.10 support line the setup stays valid. A strong print could trigger a breakout toward the next key resistance at the $81.50 50-day MA, which TLI wants to see flip to support. Otherwise TLI reiterates nothing structural has changed on the chart heading into the print.

TLI is re-highlighting REGN as an especially attractive setup, just shy of naming it a Safe Haven. After Wave 1 ran from $476 to $813, the stock pulled back in three waves and is holding the 0.618 Fib as Wave 2 support, backed by strong fundamentals and defensive positioning in a pharma sector decoupled from the broader market. TLI's verdict is an explicit Strong Buy, with Wave 3 now targeting $1166.

TSLA reclaimed the 50-week moving average near $405 this week, turning it into the key battleground bulls now need to defend. If that level holds and price can break above the $487 wedge resistance, TLI says the stock enters its Bull Case scenario, with the 1.618 Fib extension at $754 remaining the long-term target on a confirmed breakout. For now this is a wait-and-watch setup rather than a new entry.

NIO is testing the confluence of the wedge's lower support and the 0.618 Fib at $4.95. A failure to hold there opens a deeper slide to the 0.78 Fib near $4.11, which would complete Wave 2. TLI's near-term focus is simply holding within the wedge and then attempting to challenge the 50 WMA at $5.69 from below, with the Wave 3 target unchanged at the 1.618 Fib of $12.

NKE remains squeezed inside its falling wedge after briefly dipping below the 0.78 Fib near $45. Friday's 3.7% bounce was an encouraging first step, but TLI wants to see the 0.78 Fib reclaimed to validate the reversal. Failure to reclaim that level keeps a deeper pullback toward the 0.887 Fib around $28 in play, so support is not yet confirmed.

assets.entry: Holding key resistance-turned-support post-wedge breakout; downside risk to 50 DMA / 0.5 Fib
assets.target: $44 (Wave 3, aligns with Cycle Wave 3); ATH next, then 2.0–2.618 Fib ext. for Cycle Wave 3

levels.label: Key Level (holding)
levels.value: Rising wedge breakdown line still held as support

levels.label: Downside Risk
levels.value: 50 DMA / 0.5 Fib retest if support lost

assets.summary: OSCR's prior complex-correction wave count played out as expected, breaking out of its multi-month wedge and pulling back in three waves to the 0.382 Fib — which TLI now reads as a healthy Wave 2 within a strong Wave 3 uptrend rather than a bearish breakdown. Price is holding a key resistance-turned-support level and RSI is consolidating rather than washing out, both bullish signs, with UNH's Q2 beat also helping broader healthcare sentiment ahead of OSCR's own August 6th earnings. TLI's Wave 3 target sits at $44, with an eventual run at all-time highs and a further cycle-Wave-3 extension (2.0–2.618 Fib) not out of the question.

BTC is currently sitting right on its 200 WMA near $63k, and TLI says bulls should be using this level to add exposure — the same playbook already used with ETH when it dipped below its own 200 WMA. TLI calls it a no-brainer to have exposure to at least one of BTC or ETH at these depressed levels, though ETH remains its preferred pick of the two.

PEP bounced off blue support at $127 up to the 200 WMA near $170 but rejected heavily. TLI tags it 'Safe Haven soon' — a defensive name to watch as rotation into safe havens builds, but not an entry yet.

TLI expects members to rotate into Safe Havens over the coming months and keeps Palladium on watch, but the structure is still corrective. TLI advises waiting for support to confirm before treating it as an entry.

AMD keeps finding buyers exactly at the 1.618 Fib near $487, which has repeatedly held as support on every pullback. TLI's Wave 5 target remains the 2.618 Fib at $686, though it isn't ready to fully rule out a deeper Wave 4 correction first. The key thing to watch is how price behaves if the $487 zone gets retested again.

V continues to trade exactly as TLI's Elliott Wave model expected, now testing resistance at the 1.618 Fib extension near $366. A confirmed break above that level would mark Wave 5 as officially underway, with the $411 target unchanged. TLI calls the setup textbook and says no changes to the plan are needed.

levels.label: Q2 Earnings Beat
levels.value: Rev $112.0B vs $110.8B est; guidance raised to $19.50-$20.00 (from $18.25)

assets.summary: UNH beat Q2 estimates and raised full-year guidance twice, sending shares up roughly 6% premarket and pushing the stock within reach of its 200-week moving average and a +100% gain over the past year. TLI still expects a three-wave Wave 2 pullback to follow this Wave 1 run — pointing to MU's recent -40% drawdown as a reminder that even fundamentally strong names correct — and plans to add once that pullback plays out. The 50-week MA and 0.618 Fib continue to line up as the likely Wave 2 zone.

assets.entry: $11.75 (0.38 Fib + 50 Day MA confluence, Wave 4 add zone)
assets.summary: DCTH is pulling back roughly 3% toward $12.52, which is exactly the type of correction TLI wanted to see. A deeper dip into the $11.75 confluence zone (0.38 Fib plus the 50-day MA) would be the ideal add point before the stock resumes its push above Wave 3 resistance at $13.31. TLI still sees a clear technical path back to the $18 Wave 5 target and says a decision on adding is coming soon.

assets.entry: $13.00 (support retest) or $11.52–$12.34 (0.38-0.5 Fib + 50 Day MA confluence, best case)
assets.summary: ZVRA pulled back exactly to the $13 support line TLI has been watching; if it holds here, that's the next low-risk entry level. A failure of support opens the door to a deeper pullback into the $11.52–$12.34 zone, where the 0.38-0.5 Fib retracement lines up with the 50-day MA for an even stronger confluence entry. The long-term Wave 3 target remains unchanged at the 1.618 Fib near $22.

MELI remains back above the 200 WMA and is holding up better than the broader market, down only about 1% on a weak day. TLI's next focus is reclaiming the 0.38 Fib at $1,863, with the $2,000 confluence of the 50 WMA, bull-case trendline and psychological resistance acting as a magnet above that. A break above $2,000 would put the previous highs back in view.

assets.wave: Wave A/B test at amber Fib zone (within broader Wave 5)
assets.summary: NVDA is now testing the $212-$218 amber Fib zone TLI flagged as the likely ceiling for a Wave B relief bounce after yesterday's 3% drop. A rejection here would fit that read, but a clean break above $218 and then $228 would raise the odds of a Wave 5 continuation toward the 1.272 Fib target at $266. This chart matters beyond NVDA itself given its outsized influence on the broader market's own Wave 5.

TOST was added to the watchlist in early June after dropping into TLI's Wave 2 buy zone between the 0.618 and 0.786 Fib. That call has played out, with price reclaiming the 200 WMA at $27.49, and attention now shifts to a challenge of the 50 WMA at $32.91. The long-term Wave 3 target remains the 1.618 Fib at $83.

JD has bounced off its prior lows to form a double bottom, and TLI reads the recent frustration around the stock as typical sentiment near a Wave 2 low, a positive sign rather than a negative one. The next focus is reclaiming the 50-week MA at $30 and flipping it to support, which would open a lower-risk entry and ease concerns about a deeper pullback. Once $30 is reclaimed, TLI's attention turns to challenging the 200-week MA at $34 from below.

ONDS is still struggling to reclaim the 0.5 Fib at $7.94, a level TLI wants to see retaken sooner rather than later. Beyond that sits tougher confluence resistance between the 200-day MA and 50-day MA, which would need to flip to support before a move toward the bull-case line at $11.70. The stock is still inside the buy zone but carries downside risk to the 0.618 Fib at $6.20.

levels.label: Amber Fib Zone (Wave B bounce watch)

assets.summary: TLI has added an amber Fib zone between $36.84 and $37.83, flagging the possibility this is just a Wave B bounce given how short Wave 4 was — though it's equally possible the market is starting to price in excitement around HIMS's peptide business, or that Wave 3 hasn't even finished yet. Either way TLI remains comfortable holding, and would welcome a deeper, more textbook Wave 4 pullback as another add opportunity. The Wave 5 target has been refined to the 2.618 Fib at $54.

TLI notes SOFI broke above the $18.80 triangle resistance but failed to hold it through Thursday's market weakness, then rebounded strongly this week toward $19. A sustained hold above $18.80 would confirm Wave 3 is underway, with the 1.618 Fib extension at $23.40 as the long-term target.

TLI moved OPEN onto the Watchlist on a string of bullish confirmations: a standard Wave 1, a Wave 2 pullback that held the 0.618 Fib and 50 WMA, and a channel breakout now retesting the higher 0.5 Fib at $5.67, which doubles as the 50 WMA from below. Flipping that confluence level to support would put bulls in control, with TLI expecting a move to $10 next and, above that, Wave 3 confirmation toward $21. End-of-month earnings could be the catalyst, and TLI believes smart money is already accumulating.

assets.entry: $164 (current, starter-position buy) — testing $182 (200 WMA / wedge breakout) then $192 (0.618 Fib)

levels.label: Next Resistance
levels.value: $192 (0.618 Fib — clears all 3 levels to support)

levels.label: Crypto Context
levels.value: TLI's top 2 crypto picks right now: BTC & ETH

assets.summary: COIN is consolidating below its 200-week moving average at what TLI calls a very attractive $164, working toward a shared breakout/200 WMA test around $182 and the 0.618 Fib just above at $192. Clearing all three levels would flip them from resistance to support and mark a strong reversal signal. With crypto starting to trend the right way again, TLI says a starter position here makes sense for bulls wanting equity exposure to the space, though BTC and ETH remain the two cryptos TLI recommends adding to directly right now.

TLI calls HYPE's setup clean and bullish after price broke above the $59 Bull Case line and retested the confluence with the 50-day moving average twice. The near-term trigger is a breakout above $75; clearing it opens a path to the 1.618 Fib Wave 3 target of $102, provided price holds above the 50-day MA near $63.

assets.entry: $18 (200 Day MA, key support) / $22.82 (50 Day MA, resistance retest)
assets.summary: CIFR tried to clear the top of Wave 1 near $26 but slipped back below the 50-day MA at $22.82. TLI says holding the 200-day MA at $18 keeps the setup intact, with a reclaim of the 50-day MA the next step toward flipping $26 into support and confirming Bull Case status. No changes to the Wave 3 target of $53.

assets.wave: Wave 4 pullback (0.38 Fib $200 tested 4x; 0.5 Fib $175 deeper support)
assets.entry: $200 (0.38 Fib, tested 4x) / $175 (0.5 Fib, still-valid Wave 4 support)
assets.summary: NBIS continues to battle for support amid broader weakness across the data-center sector, but TLI sees no alternative count to a standard Wave 4 pullback. The 0.38 Fib at $200 has now been tested four times as bulls and bears fight it out, and a slip to the 0.5 Fib near $175 wouldn't break the structure either — a drop meaningfully below that would be the first real sign of weakness. TLI says existing holders should stay put and consider adding once strength returns.

IREN jumped 5% premarket to $40, and TLI reads the recent pullback as a textbook ABC correction into its Buy Zone, having tested the 0.78 Fib near $39 with buyers stepping back in. The next steps are reclaiming the 50-week MA at $44 as support, then breaking above the $63 Bull Case line to resume the longer-term uptrend.

assets.entry: $627 (0.5 Fib / 200 WMA confluence, projected by September)
assets.summary: TLI has mapped out an ABC corrective count for MU, with the current decline potentially completing Wave A into September if it keeps pulling back at this pace. That would put MU's correction ahead of a broader market pullback expected around the same time. The 200-week MA currently sits right at the 0.618 Fib, and if it rises to meet the 0.5 Fib near $627 by September, that confluence would mark the ideal add point; a higher high above the June peak would instead mean MU is still in Wave 5 and due for a heavier decline alongside the market.

assets.wave: Post-IPO pullback (no formal Elliott count yet)
assets.summary: SPCX has drifted lower after retesting its IPO price and bouncing to $172, with lock-up expirations feeding fear into the stock. TLI dismisses a Street price target near $800 as unrealistic given the implied market cap, and instead flags $120-$130 as the attractive entry zone, watching $147/$150 and lower amber levels of $142, $133 and $123 on the way down.

NU has pulled back in three waves to the 0.78 Fib retracement at $11.18, just above the 200-week moving average, and buyers appear to be defending that zone. TLI is now watching for a reclaim of the 50 WMA at $15 and a flip of the $16.18 Bull Case line to support to confirm the bullish scenario, with the long-term Wave 3 target unchanged at $29.

assets.entry: Add under $2,469 (200 WMA); long-term support trend line retested & held
assets.summary: ETH tested its long-term support trend line again and once more found buyers, reinforcing the floor TLI has been tracking. The 200 WMA has ticked down slightly to $2,469, and TLI expects the 50 WMA to be reclaimed fairly quickly once that level is cleared. The long-term strategy is unchanged: buy and stake ETH below the 200 WMA for anyone comfortable holding long term.

TLI has tracked this REIT since 2023, citing its low-risk business model (tenants bear operating risk), strong management, and a safe ~7% dividend. After years of stacked higher lows, a liquidity grab has now swept those lows and price sits at strong horizontal support near the 0.382 Fib inside a roughly five-year falling-wedge consolidation, which TLI views as a long-term (3-4 year) accumulation opportunity worth considering.

ALB rallied in a clean five-wave advance and has since corrected in a textbook three-wave (ABC) pullback, briefly dipping into TLI's Buy Zone between the 0.5 Fib ($134) and 0.618 Fib ($114). The near-term focus is reclaiming the 50 WMA at $137, then clearing the 200 WMA at $146, en route to a longer-term higher high above $220.

RDDT's Wave 2 pullback bottomed in textbook fashion at the 0.618 Fib near $131, and price has since strung together higher highs and higher lows, clearing the 50-week MA near $190. The next objective is the Subwave 3 target at the 1.618 Fib around $232, contingent on holding that $190 level.

assets.entry: $30 (0.78 Fib / 50 Day MA, breakout consolidation — starter position attractive)
assets.summary: CELH has broken out of its descending wedge and is now consolidating along the 0.78 Fib near $30, a pattern TLI compares to SE's own post-breakout base-building before its next leg higher. TLI expects a move toward the 200-week MA at $44 next, with earnings expected around August 11th giving the stock time to drift higher first. With healthy fundamentals and a chart that hasn't been tracking the broader market, TLI believes a starter position here makes sense.

TLI's analyst walks through two Elliott Wave counts for Ferrari, favoring a Wave 4 pullback scenario with a deeper-than-usual 0.618 retracement (justified since Wave 2 was shallow and Wave 3 ran long). Price sits below the 200-week MA with confluence support just below at the 50-week MA and 200-day MA; reclaiming that zone would set up a push toward Wave 5, framed as an interesting buy spot given Ferrari's steady growth and margins.

assets.wave: Impulse off 2021 top, now completing an ABC corrective pullback
assets.summary: TLI's morning scan flagged CTSH as one of the most oversold S&P 500 names on fundamentals, with a clean corrective ABC decline that broke the 200 WMA and is closing in on the 0.618 Fib near $36. TLI wants support to hold above that level before turning more constructive; growth is lacking but a couple of stronger earnings prints could set up a reversal back toward the 200 WMA around $70.

assets.wave: Wave 3 of impulse, following a Wave 2 correction
assets.summary: TLI describes SEZL's Wave 2 as a clean pullback that held the 0.618-0.78 Fib band before breaking out above the 0.618 Fib near $71 and flipping the 50 WMA to support, powering the stock to $171. The next key test is flipping the Wave 1 top near $186 into support, which would confirm continuation toward the 1.618 Fib extension at $350; TLI notes zero changes to this count since first charted.

assets.wave: Pullback testing 0.78 Fib support (no formal count)
assets.summary: TLI notes Richtech Robotics has pulled back to test the 0.78 Fib near $1.80, which is now needed to hold as support, with price recovering to $1.97 in premarket. TLI frames robotics as a likely next hot theme and is comfortable holding, potentially adding if the Fib support holds; a reclaim of the 50-day MA around $2.50 would confirm the pullback is over.

GRAB has reclaimed the 200 WMA at $3.91 and TLI now wants to see that level flip to support before adding further. The next hurdle is the 50 WMA near $4.60, and a clean break above it would open the Bull Case line at $5.71. The bigger picture is unchanged, with the Wave 3 target still set at the 1.618 Fib around $10.

assets.wave: 3-wave corrective pullback ahead of Wave 3 resumption
assets.entry: $4.71 (0.78 Fib), lower-risk entry near $5.67 (50-day MA flip)
assets.summary: TLI notes ASTI has corrected in three waves down to the 0.78 Fib near $4.71 while holding above the 200-day MA at $4.26, a healthy pullback for what should stay a small position given the small-cap size. The plan is to hold above the 0.78 Fib and flip the 50-day MA at $5.67 to support for a lower-risk entry, with a break above $9.81 confirming the bull case toward the Wave 3 target.

assets.wave: No formal wave count given (tweet-style call)
assets.summary: In a tweet-style post, TLI argues EBAY is headed to $180 from its current $110, framing this in the context of GME's rumored takeover interest. TLI dismisses GME's bid as unrealistic, saying GME can't afford anywhere near fair value, implying EBAY is significantly undervalued relative to TLI's target.

TLI says ABCL's Wave 2 shook out weak hands during the October market crash but the impulse count never changed. Price has made a higher high after retesting the channel breakout at $5 and is back to $7.88, confirming Wave 3 is underway, reinforcing the long-standing 1.618 Fib target.

TLI notes SIDU retraced deep to the confluence of the 0.618 Fib and 200-day MA at $2.44, then bounced 4.4% with premarket already up another 3%, showing buyers stepping in. As long as price holds above the March Wave 2 lows the chart remains valid, with the next hurdle reclaiming the 50-day MA at $3.86 as support.

assets.entry: $62 (50 WMA / Bull Case line, needs to flip to support)
assets.summary: TLI notes MP has consolidated sideways around its blue Bull Case trendline for 8 months, a level now aligning closely with the 50-week MA at $62. Flipping that level to support would confirm entry into the Bull Case, with the next target being prior highs near $100 en route to the longer-term Wave 3 target of $190.

assets.wave: Wave C retracement complete, forming next impulse
assets.entry: $102 (50 WMA, needs to flip to support)
assets.summary: TLI describes HOOD as challenging the 50-week MA at $102 from below after a textbook pattern: Wave C retraced to the 0.618 Fib, a higher low formed, and price is now testing the 50 WMA. TLI sees little to dislike on the chart — simply flipping the 50 WMA to support would open the path higher toward the all-time high at $153.

assets.entry: Above 2.618 Fib (extended Wave 5, no new entry called)
assets.summary: In a tweet, TLI highlights Goldman Sachs is up over +900% since the 2020 Covid crash low and has pushed above the 2.618 Fib, the level where Wave 5 typically completes. TLI poses whether the rally can extend further to the 3.618 Fib, implying another possible +30% move before a correction sets in.

TLI notes ACN is in a deep Wave 2 correction, coming within touching distance of the 0.78 Fib at $106. The near-term roadmap is a three-step recovery: hold above the 0.78 Fib at $106, reclaim the 0.618 Fib at $173, then target the 50-week MA at $229 from below.

assets.wave: Possible Death Cross correction (no formal count)
assets.summary: NOW remains in a cautious phase — TLI isn't yet convinced a new impulse wave has begun and is watching for the higher low to hold at the 0.618 Fib near $94; a failure there risks a deeper pullback. A Death Cross has now confirmed on the weekly chart, though TLI notes the last such cross in Feb 2023 preceded a strong rally. If the higher low holds, the next objective is the two moving averages converging near $140.

PG bounced off the 0.5/0.618 Fib support at $137 after an earlier rejection at the $165 resistance line, and is again attempting to bounce from that same level. TLI wants a break above the $150-154 WMA cluster and a flip of $165 from resistance to support before calling the Bull Case confirmed; a 2.8% dividend adds support to the thesis.

TLI describes a textbook ABC correction for Wave 2, with price breaking above the confluence of the 50 and 200 week moving averages at €11. The next hurdle is the blue resistance line at €15; a break there confirms a higher high and Wave 3 underway, targeting the 1.618 Fib at €21, with a Golden Cross also close to forming.

assets.entry: No new entry offered — price continuing steady uptrend
assets.summary: TLI notes this chart rarely needs updating since it has stayed consistent, continuing to print higher highs and higher lows. The Wave 3 target remains the 1.618 Fib at $119, with a cup formation projected to complete at the $136 resistance line, and the longer-term Wave 5 target unchanged at $165.

assets.company: HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital Inc · NYSE
assets.summary: HASI is battling the blue Bull Case line at $39, a level TLI says will be telling. A rejection here would likely mean a pullback to the 0.618 Fib/200 WMA confluence at $30, which TLI flags as the next low-risk entry; reclaiming $39 followed by a higher high above $44 opens the path to the Wave 3 target of $59.

QCOM jumped after Micron's earnings and TLI calls the pullback level very attractive, having run from $123 to $240 since April before finding support at the confluence of the wedge breakout, blue support line, and 0.5 Fib at $190. TLI is bullish, saying QCOM could be the next runner behind INTC and MU, targeting the Wave 3 level of $417 over 12-24 months.

assets.entry: $89 - $70 (0.5/0.618 Fib Buy Zone, if 200 WMA rejects)
assets.summary: TLI calls KKR a good long-term investment, with the short-term focus on whether price can flip the 200 WMA at $96 to support. A rejection there could bring a deeper pullback into the Buy Zone at the 0.5 Fib ($89) and 0.618 Fib ($70), which TLI would view as an opportunity to open a position, scaling in with multiple buys given it's still early to rule out a deeper pullback.

assets.entry: $111 (blue support line); $78-$89 secondary zone if $111 fails
assets.summary: ON dropped 11% along with other semis and TLI is watching whether buyers defend the $111 support line; if that fails, the $78-$89 zone (0.5-0.618 Fib) becomes the next support. A weekly Golden Cross is about to form (50 WMA crossing above 200 WMA), an added bullish signal ahead of the Wave 3 target.

assets.entry: $59 (confluence resistance, needs to flip to support)
assets.summary: OKLO remains inside its wedge pattern, having just retested the 0.78 Fib at $52.98. TLI wants to see price reclaim the $59 confluence level to open a path toward the 50 Day MA at $65, with a breakout above the $68 wedge resistance setting up a move to the 200 Day MA at $85 and the unchanged long-term Wave 3 target.

TLI notes DUOL has bounced roughly 50% off its lows, from $88 to $125, using it as a case study in mean reversion: buyers who accumulated at $100 or below are already sitting on sizeable gains simply from price reverting toward its 200 WMA, independent of the company's fundamentals. TLI suspects price can continue back toward that 200 WMA even from current levels.

Michael Burry disclosed a new position in DKNG, echoing TLI's own bullish read on the stock. TLI has been adding since $23 as the price ran to $30 before pulling back in what it counts as Wave 2, which has held support above the larger 0.618 Fib at $26.50. A continued hold above that level sets up a drift back to the Wave 1 high near $30 and then toward the Wave 3 target of $38.80.

assets.entry: No specific near-term entry named — broad continuation thesis
assets.target: 2.0 Fib extension (beyond reached Wave 3 target of €1,617)
assets.summary: In a video walkthrough, TLI's analyst re-examines ASML's long-term Elliott Wave structure on the Euronext Amsterdam chart, noting the stock's first meaningful pullback in years bottomed near the 0.5 Fib and bounced off the 200-week moving average, confirming Wave 2. The subsequent move has already hit the standard Wave 3 target of €1,617, and TLI thinks a further extension to the 2.0 Fib is plausible before a real Wave 4 correction arrives.

TLI reports no material change to PYPL: the plan is for price to hold above $44 support and work up to challenge the confluence of the wedge-top trendline and 50 WMA around $55. A confirmed breakout above that level would be needed before TLI turns bullish again; until then it remains a waiting game for existing holders.

TLI repeats its unchanged view on ABNB this week — price is successfully holding above the key $129 confluence of the 50 and 200 week moving averages. As long as that support level continues to hold, the next objective remains the Wave 3 target at the 1.618 Fib near $182.

MOS is rallying off Wave 2 support at $25 as Middle East supply pressure lifts fertilizer prices, and price has already reclaimed the 200-day MA at $31 within days. TLI still sees the setup as intact, preferring either a 200-day flip to support at $31 or a shallower retest of the 50-day MA at $27 as an add zone. The Wave 3 target sits at the 1.618 Fib near $49.

VSXY smashed the quarter with an EPS beat of roughly +80%, validating TLI's original thesis first shared last July at $18. Wave 2 held cleanly at the 0.5 Fib and 200-day MA around $40, and the share price has since ripped from $40 to $75 in two weeks. TLI leaves the Wave 3 target at $128 and the eventual Wave 5 target at $181 unchanged.

TLI flags a very bullish setup on Cocoa futures: price has held a strong ascending triangle for months and is now testing the breakout level at $4,800. He believes once $4,800 flips to support, price can run cleanly to the 200 WMA at $5,600 before meeting the larger channel resistance for the next breakout test. Flagged as a name worth watching for members trading futures via Plus500.

assets.entry: $108-$113 (50 WMA / 0.5 Fib confluence, next Wave 2 entry)
assets.summary: TGT has rallied strongly and TLI wants to see it test resistance at the 200 WMA near $131 to complete Wave 1 before a pullback into Wave 2. The 50 WMA is curling higher toward $108 and converging with the 0.5 Fib at $113, and TLI flags that confluence as the next low-risk entry once the pullback arrives. The Wave 3 target sits at the 1.618 Fib extension near $187, and the stock also carries a healthy 3.4% dividend.

DASH is showing strength after Wave C held support at the confluence of the 0.5-0.618 Fib and the 200 WMA. Price is now printing higher highs and higher lows, and TLI is watching for a hold above the 50 WMA at $206 to confirm a move toward the next resistance at $257. TLI reads this as Wave 1 of a new Primary Wave 3, with a long-term target of $539.

SLNH has pulled back in three controlled waves within a bullish descending wedge to the 0.618 Fib, where TLI wants to see support hold — a level also attracting buying from known investor Mike Alfred. A hold here sets up a move back to the wedge breakout zone between $1.57 and $1.63, where the 50 and 200-day MAs converge, with a confirmed break above that range opening the path to the Wave 3 target of $4.

assets.entry: 0.786 Fib retracement + declining wedge + 50-day MA confluence
assets.summary: After a textbook 5-wave impulse to all-time highs, LULU corrected in an A-B-C decline that briefly violated the wave-1 high, an unusual structure. Price now sits at the 0.786 Fib retracement in a declining wedge with 50-day MA confluence, and TLI is watching for a capitulation bounce with a higher high and higher low before treating it as a bottom.

GPC has spent almost four years in a long Wave 2 base and TLI is now watching for a strong Wave 3 breakout. The key test is the confluence of the wedge breakout and the 200 WMA at $139 - a flip to support opens a move to $187, and a break above $187 would confirm Wave 3 toward $317. Bulls may open a small starter position now at the lower-risk entry.

assets.company: Vanguard FTSE Canadian High Dividend Yield ETF
assets.summary: After nearly two years of Wave 2 consolidation, VDY broke out into what TLI is treating as Wave 3, now approaching the 1.618 Fib near $79. A rejection there could justify trimming 20-30% of a position, while those looking to add should watch for a Wave 4 pullback toward $64. TLI calls the chart textbook so far.

FCX has a strong confluence level at the top of Wave 1 with the 50 WMA at $53, and a retest here would offer bulls another buying opportunity. The 1 Fib at $71 has acted as resistance multiple times and needs to flip to support to confirm Wave 3 is underway, with a longer-term target at the 1.618 Fib near $101.

AVAV's Wave C decline dropped below the 200 WMA to the 0.78 Fib at $124 before buyers stepped in, and price has spent two weeks challenging the 200 WMA at $167. TLI is watching for that level to flip to support and a break above the 0.618 Fib at $187 to confirm the Bull Case, which would target the 50 WMA at $251 next.

TLI is turning increasingly bullish on metals into September, and unlike silver's parabolic move, copper has stayed in a more controlled, steady Wave 3 uptrend over the last two years. A flip of $6.50 to support would confirm the next leg toward the 1.618 Fib target near $8, and TLI calls it a solid chart for the next 18 months.

assets.wave: Wave A complete (5 waves down) → awaiting Wave B
assets.entry: $534 (0.5 Fib / 200 Day MA / 50 Day MA confluence) - Wave B bounce/trim zone
assets.summary: TLI counts five waves down on INTU, consistent with a completed Wave A. The focus now shifts to a Wave B relief bounce toward $534, a level reinforced by the 0.5 Fib and confluence with both the 200-day and 50-day MAs, where resistance and the start of Wave C are expected. Since Wave B typically unfolds in three waves (ABC), TLI flags this bounce as a second chance for existing holders to trim before the next leg lower.

levels.label: Note
levels.value: Trim opportunity into bounce, Wave C to follow

assets.summary: QQQ has held a controlled, bullish ascending wedge since early June, using the 50-day moving average as support the entire way, which TLI reads as a healthy setup rather than a warning sign. A breakout above the wedge and the $732 resistance level would confirm the final leg of Wave 5, and could be a strong move if Q3 earnings season matches Q1's strength, echoing last September's rally. TLI cautions that a Wave 2-style pullback (similar to what hit $NBIS starting in October) remains a real risk once that breakout happens.

assets.company: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust · NYSE Arca
assets.summary: TLI's weekly market outlook flags a heavy economic calendar (CPI, Retail Sales, Fed Chair Warsh testimony) as the near-term catalyst, but the bigger concern is narrow leadership: fewer than 15 mega-cap tech names are propping up the index while many stocks, including $MSFT, have already slipped back to their 200-week MA. TLI expects AI capex spending to keep outpacing any visible ROI through Q3 earnings, and believes this disappointment starts tipping the broader market into decline from around September. The advice is to avoid adding to positions that have simply ridden this year's Wave 5 rally and instead keep hedges and defensive havens in place.

assets.entry: 50 WMA support (repeatedly held, price ~20% below 200 WMA)
assets.summary: UPS is still roughly 20% below its 200 WMA near $135, but has repeatedly held support at the 50 WMA with buyers stepping in convincingly, producing a sequence of higher highs and higher lows consistent with early Wave 3. TLI calls it one of the safer opportunities in the market right now, helped by a ~5.8% dividend yield, with the Wave 3 target set at $159.

assets.wave: Testing 50 WMA resistance, pre-Bull Case breakout
assets.summary: META is challenging the 50 WMA at $658 from below, which TLI says is normal and not a concern. A clean break above the $680 breakout level would put META back into Bull Case territory with the next resistance at $741. If $680 flips to support and $741 is subsequently cleared, TLI sees the Bullish target at the 0.618 Fib near $914 coming into play.

assets.entry: $14.91 (Bull/Bear Case line, needs flip to support)
assets.summary: CLSK jumped +20% premarket after signing a 20-year, $6.6B lease (up to $11.6B with extension options) for a Sandersville, GA data-center campus deploying 175MW of IT load starting Q4 2027. The move brings price right to the $14.91 Bull/Bear Case battleground level that's been fought over since Feb 2024; flipping it to support opens a run to $23, and clearing $23 targets the top blue line at $42.

assets.wave: 3-wave pullback complete from $155 high; 200 WMA flip test
assets.entry: $57 (200 WMA / historical blue support-resistance line, flipping to support)

levels.label: Strategy
levels.value: Starter position now; add more once $57 confirmed as support

assets.summary: RBLX has completed a three-wave pullback from its recent high near $155 and is now working to flip the 200-week moving average — also a historical blue support/resistance line — to support around $57. TLI calls this an attractive Buy Zone level for a starter position, with plans to add more once $57 is confirmed as support. Earnings on July 30th could be a catalyst, with Roblox having beaten in each of its last three reports.
